{{Quote|No, you must never trust them. They'll ply you with cheap Mandalorian wine and whisper sweet nothings while they stab you in the back.|[[Trymbo]]|<ref name="TOR" />}}
'''Mandalorian wine''' was a type of [[Alcohol/Legends|alcoholic]] beverage made by members of the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] culture.<ref name="Bane" /> Also known as ''mando papuur'gal'' in the [[Mando'a/Legends|''Mando'a'']] [[Language/Legends|language]],<ref name="Mando'a">{{WebCite|url=http://www.karentraviss.com/page20/page26/index.html|text=Mando'a|author=Karen Traviss|work=[http://www.karentraviss.com Karen Traviss]|archivedate=20110925083955}}</ref> Mandalorian [[Wine/Legends|wine]] was created from a combination of [[Fruit/Legends|fruit]] and wild [[Rice/Legends|rice]]. The drink was typically very strong, with a slightly bitter taste, and possessed a deep red [[Color/Legends|coloration]].<ref name="Galaxies" />

Mandalorian wine gained a notable increase in popularity in the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] following the end of the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. By the time of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]], Mandalorian wine was often kept corked in a [[Glass/Legends|glass]] decanter prior to consumption.<ref name="Galaxies" /> An individual who had become intoxicated from Mandalorian wine was commonly known to move with a stumbling gait.<ref name="Bane" />

==Behind the scenes==
Mandalorian wine first appeared in ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]] in the [[Star Wars video games|MMORPG]], ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', released [[June 26]], [[2003]]. The drink was later mentioned in the [[2009]] novel, ''[[Darth Bane: Dynasty of Evil]]'', by [[Author/Legends|author]] [[Drew Karpyshyn]].
